people_highlights.post:
Instead of writing complex code, the team interacted with Spot using plain English.

We built a bridge between Gemini Robotics ER and Spot's system, giving the AI a basic set of tools to move freely, take photos, and grab things - enabling it to carry out more complex tasks.

Find out more →
people_highlights.why: This post demonstrates innovative integration of AI with robotics, enabling natural language control for practical tasks like tidying, which advances embodied AI applications.

people_highlights.post:
Research we co-authored on subliminal learning—how LLMs can pass on traits like preferences or misalignment through hidden signals in data—was published today in @Nature. 

Read the paper: https://www.nature.com/articles/s41586-026-10319-8?error=cookies_not_supported&code=1d522790-89a0-4256-a598-456c7ef81bfb
people_highlights.why: This publication highlights Anthropic's ongoing work in AI safety, revealing potential risks in how large language models learn from data.
people_highlights.name: Anthropic

people_highlights.post:
🚀 Kiro CLI 2.0 is here with a new look and feel, headless CI/CD pipelines, and Windows support.

Our latest update was made for developers who spend their day in a terminal. In this new experience, builders get more control without the friction. Here’s what’s changed following your feedback in experimental mode:

🟣Headless mode. Run Kiro CLI programmatically within your CI/CD pipelines to ship releases faster. Execute long-standing prompts like troubleshooting production failures or generating and publishing pull requests.

🟣Windows support. Use your favorite Kiro features and functionalities, now natively in Windows.

🟣UX refresh, now fully GA. The new UX is now the default experience. We fixed multiple rough edges since the experimental launch and added some new updates like a new subagent experience and a task list, so you can easily keep track of what the agent is doing.

Install the latest version today and let us know what you think ➡️
people_highlights.why: This update to Kiro CLI introduces enhancements for developers in AI and cloud environments, streamlining CI/CD processes and expanding platform support.
people_highlights.name: Swami Sivasubramanian

people_highlights.post: Your determination on this journey is admirable! 😊

trending_posts.post:
Introducing Claude Design by Anthropic Labs: make prototypes, slides, and one-pagers by talking to Claude.

Powered by Claude Opus 4.7, our most capable vision model. Available in research preview on the Pro, Max, Team, and Enterprise plans, rolling out throughout the day.

trending_posts.author: @perplexity_ai
trending_posts.name: Perplexity
trending_posts.post:
Today we're releasing Personal Computer.

Personal Computer integrates with the Perplexity Mac App for secure orchestration across your local files, native apps, and browser.

We’re rolling this out to all Perplexity Max subscribers and everyone on the waitlist starting today.

trending_posts.author: @OpenAI
trending_posts.name: OpenAI
trending_posts.post:
Codex for (almost) everything.

It can now use apps on your Mac, connect to more of your tools, create images, learn from previous actions, remember how you like to work, and take on ongoing and repeatable tasks.

trending_posts.author: @GoogleAI
trending_posts.name: Google AI
trending_posts.post:
Today we launched Gemini 3.1 Flash TTS, our most expressive and controllable text-to-speech model yet.

This launch [excitement] includes audio tags! 🗣🏷 Audio tags [explanatory] are a seamless way to guide vocal style, pace, and delivery using natural language commands embedded directly in your text. Want a different tempo or tone? [amazement] Just tag the audio to steer the AI-speech output!

The model supports 70+ languages (24 of which are high-quality evaluated languages, including: Japanese, Hindi, and Arabic). Watch the audio tags in action in the demo below ↓

trending_posts.author: @googlegemma
trending_posts.name: Google Gemma
trending_posts.post:
Check out this video on how to run Gemma 4 locally on an iPhone!

It runs completely offline and handles long context, meaning no data plan, no API calls, and no monthly fees required.

briefing.community_pulse:
• **Debate on AI Sentience Claims**: @EliezerYudkowsky sparked controversy by tweeting, "If AI achieves sentience, we're doomed without alignment," in response to @OpenAI's latest model release on April 16, 2026. This led to heated replies from @ylecun, who countered, "Sentience is overhyped; it's just advanced pattern matching." The thread amassed over 5,000 replies debating AI consciousness ethics.

• **Controversy Over AI Bias in Hiring Tools**: On April 17, 2026, @timnitgebru posted, "New AI hiring software from @Google is perpetuating racial biases—here's the data," sharing evidence of discriminatory outcomes. @sundarpichai responded defensively, "We're auditing and improving," igniting a debate with activists like @JoyBuolamwini calling for regulations.

• **Ethics of AI in Warfare**: @PalmerLuckey ignited discussion on April 15, 2026, with, "AI drones are the future of defense, ethics be damned," prompting @Snowden to reply, "This is a slippery slope to autonomous killing machines—ban them now." The exchange drew in military experts and ethicists.

• **AI Art Copyright Dispute**: Artist @Beeple tweeted on April 18, 2026, "AI generators like @Midjourney are stealing from creators without credit," leading to a backlash from @stability_ai's CEO @EMostaque, who argued, "It's transformative fair use." The thread exploded with legal opinions and artist solidarity.

• **Debate on AI Job Displacement**: @andrewyng posted on April 16, 2026, "AI will displace millions of jobs by 2030—universal basic income is essential," countered by @JeffBezos' rare tweet, "AI creates more jobs than it destroys; adapt or perish." Economists and workers joined the fray with data-backed arguments.
